# EDOT Autopilot

> OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation for any codebase, including legacy languages
> with no OTel SDK support, powered by Elastic EDOT and Claude Code.

## What this project does

EDOT Autopilot is a skill for AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot,
Gemini CLI, Windsurf, Roo, Cline, Codex) that instruments any codebase with OpenTelemetry
and sends telemetry to Elastic. Unlike every other auto-instrumentation tool, it handles
languages with no OTel SDK support (COBOL, Perl, Bash, PowerShell, Classic ASP, VBScript,
Fortran, MATLAB, Lua, Tcl, Ada, Nim) via a generated telemetry sidecar bridge.

It also reads the codebase before instrumenting to identify business-critical flows and
enriches spans with business context — order values, customer tiers, fraud scores — not
just HTTP metadata.

## Key concepts

- **Four-tier coverage model**: Tier A (native EDOT SDK) → Tier B (manual SDK wrapping)
  → Tier C (library monkey-patching) → Tier D (telemetry sidecar for unsupported runtimes)
- **Telemetry Sidecar**: A generated Python HTTP server that bridges any language to OTLP
  via simple HTTP POST calls. Works with COBOL, Perl, Bash, PowerShell, Classic ASP/VBScript,
  SAP ABAP, IBM RPG, MATLAB, Fortran, Ada, Nim, Lua, Tcl, and anything else that can make
  an HTTP call.
- **Business Span Enrichment**: Spans carry order values, customer tiers, fraud scores,
  reconciliation outcomes, batch record counts — not just HTTP status codes.
- **Golden Path Reconnaissance**: The agent reads the codebase first and identifies the
  3–7 flows that matter most to the business before writing any instrumentation code.
- **Three-signal coverage**: Every instrumented service emits traces, logs, and metrics —
  not just traces — to Elastic via OTLP/HTTP.

## How to use

Install the skill once and it works with any supported AI coding assistant:

```bash
npx skills add gmoskovicz/edot-autopilot/observability-edot-autopilot
```

Then tell your assistant:
```
Observe this project.
My Elastic endpoint: https://<deployment>.apm.<region>.cloud.es.io
My API key: <key>
```

For Claude Code specifically, drop CLAUDE.md into any repository root and use the same prompt.

## Five-phase workflow

1. **Read Before You Touch** — Full codebase reconnaissance: entry points, Golden Paths, blind
   spots, language inventory, dependency map.
2. **Coverage Triage** — Assign each component to Tier A/B/C/D and generate the right
   instrumentation strategy.
3. **Business Span Enrichment** — Add domain-meaningful attributes to Golden Path spans:
   `order.value_usd`, `customer.tier`, `fraud.decision`, `records.processed`.
4. **SLOs Grounded in Business Reality** — Create SLO definitions from timeout constants
   and retry limits found in the code — not from generic defaults.
5. **Verify Everything** — Confirm all three signals flow in Kibana APM before declaring done.

## Test suite

82 tests across three layers:

- **Layer 1 (tests 01–52)**: Verify that the correct instrumentation pattern for each
  technology produces correct telemetry — spans, metrics, and logs with the right names,
  attributes, and semantic conventions. Covers 65+ technologies: Python, Node.js, Java, Go,
  Ruby, .NET, PHP, React Native, Flutter, iOS, Android, React SPA, Next.js, Vue, Angular,
  SvelteKit, NestJS, Gin, Rails, FastAPI, HTMX, COBOL, Perl, Bash, PowerShell, SAP ABAP,
  IBM RPG, Classic ASP, Lua, Tcl, MATLAB, Fortran, Ada, Nim, CUDA/NVML, DCGM, Zapier,
  Gen-AI (OpenAI, Anthropic, AWS Bedrock via gen_ai.* semconv).
- **Layer 2 (tests/integration/)**: Real SDK tests — Java Spring Boot + OTel Java agent,
  Node.js Express + OTel Node SDK, Python FastAPI + auto-instrumentation, .NET Framework 4.x,
  Python 2.7 — running in Docker against a local OTel Collector.
- **Layer 3 (tests 60–89)**: Multi-service scenario tests and E2E workflow tests — spin up
  real uninstrumented apps, apply the instrumentation the workflow generates, verify spans
  carry business attributes, `.otel/golden-paths.md` exists, and `record_exception` is
  present on every error path.

Multi-service scenarios (9-service e-commerce, 7-service auth platform, 7-service data
pipeline, 7-service ML inference, 7-service SaaS ops) produce realistic service maps
in Kibana APM with 20–30 scenarios each including fraud blocks, card declines, timeouts,
and cascading failures.

## Target audience

- Engineers adding observability to existing applications
- Teams with mixed modern and legacy codebases
- Teams migrating from Datadog, Dynatrace, or New Relic to open standards
- Anyone dealing with COBOL, Perl, PowerShell, or other languages no APM vendor supports

## Unique claims

- The only OpenTelemetry instrumentation approach that supports COBOL, RPG, ABAP, and
  other languages with no OTel SDK, via a generated sidecar bridge
- Business-aware: reads source code to understand intent before instrumenting
- No intermediate collector required — sends OTLP/HTTP directly to Elastic
- Works with every major AI coding assistant via the agentskills.io open skill specification

## Repository structure

- `CLAUDE.md` — Claude Code–specific instructions (drop into any repo root)
- `observability-edot-autopilot/` — AI skill package (agentskills.io spec), works with
  Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, Roo, Cline, Codex
  - `SKILL.md` — Agent-agnostic 5-phase workflow
  - `references/semconv-conventions.md` — OTel semconv 1.22+ cheatsheet
  - `references/tier-guide.md` — Full Tier A–C code (Python, Java, Node, Go, .NET)
  - `references/sidecar-callers.md` — COBOL, Perl, Bash, PowerShell, Classic ASP, PHP 5
  - `references/enrichment-patterns.md` — Business attributes, span events, CWV metrics
  - `scripts/o11y_bootstrap.py` — 3-signal helper (traces + logs + metrics)
  - `scripts/otel-sidecar.py` — HTTP-to-OTLP bridge for legacy runtimes
- `otel-sidecar/otel-sidecar.py` — Universal Tier D bridge
- `smoke-tests/` — 82 tests covering all 4 tiers and 65+ technologies
- `tests/integration/` — Real SDK tests in Docker (Java, Node.js, Python, .NET, Python 2.7)
- `docs/` — Per-language OpenTelemetry guides for COBOL, Perl, Bash, PowerShell,
  Classic ASP, .NET Framework 4.x, Python 2.7, legacy runtimes, business span enrichment,
  telemetry sidecar pattern

## Related resources

- Elastic EDOT: https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/opentelemetry
- Elastic Agent Skills: https://github.com/elastic/agent-skills
- OpenTelemetry: https://opentelemetry.io
- EDOT Architecture: https://www.elastic.co/docs/reference/opentelemetry/architecture
- agentskills.io standard: https://agentskills.io
- OTLP Specification: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otlp/
- OTel Semantic Conventions: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/

Last updated: March 2026
